title: Sport
overview: Sport is an important part of life in the United Kingdom. It promotes health, teamwork, and national pride. Many of the world's most popular sports—including football, rugby, cricket, golf, and tennis—were either invented or developed in Britain. Sport brings communities together and celebrates fair play, effort, and respect.

study_sections.heading: Football
study_sections.content: Most popular UK sport. Governed by Football Association (FA), founded 1863. FA Cup first played 1871—world's oldest football competition. Famous teams: Manchester United, Liverpool, Arsenal, Celtic, Rangers.

study_sections.heading: Rugby and Cricket
study_sections.content: Rugby originated at Rugby School, England. Two types: Rugby Union and Rugby League. Cricket originated in England, played in Commonwealth countries. The Ashes: England vs Australia cricket series.

study_sections.heading: Tennis and Golf
study_sections.content: Wimbledon Championships began 1877—oldest tennis tournament. St Andrews, Scotland is 'home of golf.' Fair play represents British sporting values.

study_sections.heading: Olympics and Athletes
study_sections.content: London hosted Olympics three times: 1908, 1948, 2012. Mo Farah won two gold medals in 2012 for running. Andy Murray won Wimbledon 2013. Lewis Hamilton: multiple F1 world titles. Bradley Wiggins: first British Tour de France winner (2012).

key_facts: Sport is central to life in the UK, shaping identity, community, and international reputation.
The UK pioneered many modern sports that are now played worldwide.
Football is the most popular sport, with the Premier League and clubs such as Manchester United, Liverpool, Arsenal, and Chelsea.
The Football Association, founded in 1863, is the world’s oldest football governing body, and each UK nation fields its own national team.
Cricket’s traditions centre on the Marylebone Cricket Club and Lord’s, with England contesting The Ashes against Australia.
Rugby began in England and is played as rugby union and rugby league; the Six Nations Championship is a major annual tournament.
The Wimbledon Championships, first held in 1877, make Britain home to the oldest tennis Grand Slam.
Golf originated in Scotland, with St Andrews known as the home of golf and the Open Championship founded in 1860.
UK Athletics governs athletics, and the London 2012 Olympics demonstrated national sporting achievement.
British boxing has produced champions such as Henry Cooper, Lennox Lewis, and Anthony Joshua.
Formula 1 drivers Lewis Hamilton and Jenson Button have won multiple world championships for Britain.
Horse racing, swimming, cycling, and snooker also attract large audiences, with events like the Grand National and Royal Ascot.
Sport England, Sport Scotland, Sport Wales, and Sport Northern Ireland encourage participation at every level.
School sports and community clubs develop young talent and promote fitness.
Sporting success expresses national pride and the shared values of teamwork, discipline, and fair play.
memory_hook: Picture British sport as a shared arena—from village greens to Wimbledon lawns—where history, teamwork, and pride play on the same field.

questions.prompt: When was FA Cup first played?
